questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A square planar complex is formed by hybridization of which atomic orbitals ?
questions.solution:
Square planer geometry involve one s, two p, one d orbital having dsp2 hybridization as below:

S + Px + Py + dx2 - y2

All four hybridized orbitals in the XY Plane.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: Which of the following hybridization pattern best describes the carbon atom in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>NCO</mi><mo>-</mo></msup></math>?
questions.solution: NCO- is isoelectronic with CO2, which has a linear geometry in which C-atom is sp-hybridized.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Find out the bond order in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>He</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><msubsup><mi>He</mi><mn>2</mn><mo>+</mo></msubsup><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msubsup><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>2</mn><mo>+</mo></msubsup></math>, respectively
questions.solution:
Bond order =bonding electrons -anti bonding electrons2

He2 bond order 

electronic configuration = 1s2 or 1s2    

MOT configuration = σ1s2σ1s*2

BO= 2-22=0

He2+ bond order =

electronic configuration = 1s2 or 1s1

 MOT connfiguration=σ1s2σ1s*1

BO=2-12=0.5

O2 bond order 

electronic configuration =1s2 2s22p4 or 1s2 2s22p4

MOT configuration=KKLLσ2pz2 π2px2= π2py2  π2p*x1 = π2p*y1

BO=6-22=2

O+2 bond order 

electronic configuration =1s2 2s22p4 or 1s2 2s22p3

MOT configuration= KKLLσ2pz2 π2px2= π2py2  π2p*x1 = π2p*0   

BO=6-12=2.5
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: The cyanide ion <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><msup><mi>CN</mi><mo>-</mo></msup></mfenced></math> and the nitrogen molecule <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></mfenced></math> are isoelectronic. However, in contrast to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>CN</mi><mo>-</mo></msup><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> is chemically inert due to:
options: Presence of greater number of electrons in bonding
questions.solution:
CN- ion in a free state is very reactive.

N ≡ N (N2 molecule)

Bond dissociation energy is very high, hence, it is chemically inert.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Dipole moment of $\mathrm{HCl}=1.03 \mathrm{D}, \mathrm{HI}=0.38 \mathrm{D}$. Bond length of $\mathrm{HCl}=1.3 Ã…$ and $\mathrm{HI}=1.6 Ã…$. The ratio of fraction of electric charge, $\delta$, existing on each atom in $\mathrm{HCl}$ and $\mathrm{HI}$ is
questions.solution:
From the definition of dipole moment,
$\mu=\delta \times d$
where, $\delta=$ magnitude of electric charge
$d=$ distance between particles (here bond length)
$\begin{array}{rlrl}\therefore & \delta =\frac{\mu}{d} \\ \text { or, } \quad \frac{\delta_{\mathrm{HCl}}}{\delta_{\mathrm{HI}}} & =\frac{\mu_{\mathrm{HCl}}}{d_{\mathrm{HCl}}} \times \frac{d_{\mathrm{HI}}}{\mu_{\mathrm{HI}}} \\ & =\frac{1.03 \times 1.6}{1.3 \times 0.38}=3.3: 1\end{array}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: Which of the following is incorrect statement about OSF<sub>4</sub>?
options: OSF<sub>4</sub> has distorted trigonal bipyramidal shape.
O atom is present at equatorial position.
There is no lone pair on S.
questions.solution:
In OSF4, BP = 5, LP = 0 and hence the hybridization is sp3 d and shape is trigonal-bipyramidal.



Distortion in geometry due to presence of S=O (Bond) equatorially. S-atom does not contain any lone pair electrons.Distortion in geometry due to presence of S=O (Bond) equatorially. S-atom does not contain any lone pair electrons.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Which of the following has least bond energy?
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} \mathrm{N}_{2}^{2-}=K K^{\prime} \sigma 2 s^{2}, \sigma 2 s^{2}, \sigma 2 p_{z}^{2}, \pi 2 p_{x}^{2}, \approx \pi & 2 p_{y}^{2} \\ & \pi 2 p_{x}^{2} \end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} \therefore \text { Bond order } &=\frac{N_{b}-N_{a}}{2} \\ &=\frac{8-4}{2}=2 \end{aligned}$
(b) $\mathrm{N}_{2}^{-}=K K^{\prime} \sigma 2 \mathrm{~s}^{2}, \sigma 2 \mathrm{~s}^{2}, \sigma 2 p_{z}^{2}, \pi 2 p_{x}^{2}=\pi 2 p_{y}^{2}$
Bond order $=\frac{8-3}{2}=2.5$
(c) $\mathrm{N}_{2}^{+}=K K^{\prime} \sigma 2 s^{2} \sigma 2 s^{2}, \pi 2 p_{x}^{2}=\pi 2 p_{y}^{2}, \sigma 2 p_{z}^{1}$
Bond order $=\frac{7-2}{2}=2.5$
(d) $\mathrm{N}_{2}=K K^{\prime} \sigma 2 s^{2}, \sigma^{\dagger} 2 s^{2}, \pi 2 p_{x}^{2}=\pi 2 p_{y}^{2}, \sigma 2 p_{z}^{2}$
Bond order $=\frac{8-2}{2}=3$
$\because$ Bond energy $\propto \frac{1}{\text { Bond order }}$
$\therefore \mathrm{N}_{2}$ has least bond energy.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: Hybridisations of carbon- 2 in $P$ and $Q$ are respectively.
questions.solution:
In the reaction,
The hybridisation of carbon-2 in $(P)$ is $s p^3$ and in $(Q)$ in $s p^2$, because of 4 sigma bonds in $(P)$ and one pi $(\pi)$ bond in $(Q)$.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Which of the following has highest boiling point?
questions.solution:
NaCl solution likely has the highest boiling point among the options listed due to the ionic nature of NaCl and its impact on increasing the boiling point significantly.
The boiling points of urea solution and sucrose solution would be higher than that of pure water but lower than that of the NaCl solution.
The boiling point of solution (4) would depend on the nature and concentration of the solute, so without more information, it's challenging to determine its boiling point relative to the others.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: When two compounds <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>ACl</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>DCl</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math> of two elements <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">D</mi></math> are mixed together, a compound <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>ADCl</mi><mn>6</mn></msub></math> is formed. The structural analysis showed that <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>ADCl</mi><mn>6</mn></msub></math> is an ionic compound. Given that <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>DCl</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math> is trigonal planar and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>ACl</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math> is trigonal pyramidal, predict the shape of the cation if the shape of the anion is tetrahedral.
questions.solution:
⇒ Since, DCl3 is trigonal planar, hence, 'D' belongs to the boron family.

⇒ Since ACl3 is trigonal pyramidal, hence, 'A' belongs to the nitrogen family.

⇒ DCl3+ACl3→DCl2+ ACl4- or ACl2+DCl4-

If the anionic part is ACl4- which has a see-saw structure, then, cationic part will be DCl2+ where the hybridization of D will be sp and hence, the shape will be linear.

If the anionic part is DCl4- i.e., it is of tetrahedral shape, then, the cationic part will be ACl2+ which will be bent with sp2 hybridisation.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The correct sequence of bond order is
questions.solution:
According to molecular orbital theory

Bond order =(number of e- in bonding molecular orbital) -(number of e- in Anti bonding Molecular orbital)2
Molecular electronic configuration :-

O2+=σ1s2<σ*1s2<σ2s2<σ*2s2<σ2px2<π2py2=π2pz2<π*2py1=π*2pz0O2=σ1s2<σ*1s2<σ2s2<σ*2s2<σ2px2<π2py2=π2pz2<π*2py1=π*2pz1O2-=σ1s2<σ*1s2<σ2s2<σ*2s2<σ2px2<π2py2=π2pz2<π*2py2=π*2pz1O22-=σ1s2<σ*1s2<σ2s2<σ*2s2<σ2px2<π2py2=π2pz2<π*2py2=π*2pz2

Bond order of the species :-

O2+=B.O.=6-12=2.5O2=B.O.=6-22=2O2-=B.O.=6-32=1.5O22-=B.O.=6-42=1

Hence, the correct order of bond order is O2+>O2>O2->O22-.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Which of the following is TRUE regarding hybridisation in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mtext>PCl</mtext><mtext>5</mtext></msub></math>?
options: All the bond angles in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mtext>PCl</mtext><mtext>5</mtext></msub></math>  are equivalent.
Axial and equatorial bonds have the same bond length.

On orbital overlap between phosphorus and chlorine, five <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mtext>sp</mtext><mtext>3</mtext></msup><mtext> d-p</mtext></math> sigma  covalent bonds are formed.
Axial and equatorial bonds have equivalent bond energies.
questions.solution:
On orbital overlap between  phosphorus and chlorine, five sp3 d-p sigma covalent bonds are formed. This statement is true, as PCl5 carries five sigma bonds and all these sigma bonds are used for hybridisation according to the valence shell electron pair repulsion theory. Structure of PCl5 is shown below:

And we can see here, that axial bonds and equatorial bonds, both are not of the same length.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Identify the correct statements from the following.
(1) The dipole moment of $\mathrm{CO}_2$ and $\mathrm{BF}_3$ is zero.
(2) The dipole moment of $\mathrm{NF}_3$ is higher than the dipole moment of $\mathrm{NH}_3$.
(3) The dipole moment of $\mathrm{HI}$ is lower than the dipole moment of $\mathrm{HCl}$.
questions.solution:
1 . The $\mathrm{BF}_3$ molecule has a symmetrical trigonal planar geometry, the moment of any two B-F dipoles is equal in magnitude but opposite in direction to the moment of the $\mathrm{BF}_3$ molecule is zero. Same as $\mathrm{CO}_2$. 2. In $\mathrm{NH}_3, \mathrm{~N}$ is more electronegative than $\mathrm{H}$ so, $\mathrm{N}$ pulls the electrons from $\mathrm{H}$ towards itself so, the direction of moment due to the $\mathrm{N}-\mathrm{H}$ bonds is in the same direction as that of the lone pair of electrons on nitrogen.
But in case of $\mathrm{NF}_3, \mathrm{H}-\bullet$ [Ione pair] moment is in the opposite direction to the $\mathrm{N}-\mathrm{F}$ bond moments. Thus, has smaller net dipole moment. 3. $\because$ Size of $\mathrm{I}$-atom is more than of CI-atom and electronegativity of I-atom is less than of CI-atom. Hence, HI has Iower dipole-moment than HCI.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

As we move down to the group, the size of the central metal ion will increase and due to this the bond pair-bond pair repulsion will go on decreasing and therefore the two small hydrogen come closer to each other and so it causes decrease in H-M-H bond angle and hence the bond angle will decrease down to the group as shown below : 

H2O>H2S>H2Se>H2Te

questions.q: Which of the following has the smallest bond angle <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi><mo>-</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><mo>-</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi><mo>)</mo></math> in the given molecules? <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi></math> represents a halogen atom.
questions.solution:
All the given compounds have similar basic geometry:

  

Where, X=F, Cl, Br, I

The difference in X-S-X bond angle is due to the difference in electronegativity of X atom. This is because, if the electronegativity of X is more, then the shared pair of electrons will be closer to X and will be at more distance from each other which will cause less repulsions, thus, will have smaller X-S-X bond angle.

Out of F, Cl, Br and I, Fluorine is the most electronegative, thus, will have minimum F-S-F bond angle.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: What type of inter molecular force is present between magnesium chloride and water?
questions.solution:
Ion-dipole interaction
Between magnesium chloride and water, there is an ion-dipole interaction. This contact is caused by polar water molecules interacting with a magnesium ion. The water molecule's oxygen atom contains a tiny negative charge, which attracts the positively charged magnesium ion. These are substantially weaker forces than covalent or ionic bonding.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Group the molecules/ions according to bond order.
questions.solution:
Bond order of the group
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{O}_2^{-} \text {has }=1, \quad \mathrm{Li}_2 \text { has }=1 \\
& \mathrm{~F}_2 \text { has }=1, \quad \mathrm{He}_2^{2+} \text { has }=1 \\
&
\end{aligned}
$$
Similarly, $\mathrm{N}_2$ has B. $\mathrm{O}=3$ and $\mathrm{O}_2^{2+}$ has B.O $=3$, i.e. all the species $\left(\mathrm{O}_2^{-} \mathrm{Li}_2, \mathrm{~F}_2\right.$ and $\left.\mathrm{He}_2^{2+}\right)$ has $\mathrm{B} . \mathrm{O}=1$ and both the numbers $\left(\mathrm{N}_2\right.$ and $\left.\mathrm{O}_2^{2+}\right)$ has B.O ' 3 '. Hence, these can be grouped together.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: In which of the following salts, the solubility increases appreciably with increase in temperature?
questions.solution:
The solubility of solid solute depends upon temperature.
Variation of solubilities of some ionic solids with temperature - Generally, rise in temperature increase the solubility. This is due to expansion of holes or empty spaces in the liquid solvent. Generally, $10^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ rise in temperature, increase the solubility of solids two-fold. - Dissolution process may be endothermic or exothermic. - The solubility of the substances like $\mathrm{NaBr}, \mathrm{NaCl}, \mathrm{KCl}$, etc. changes slightly with the increase in temperature. - The solubility of the salts like $\mathrm{NaNO}_3, \mathrm{KNO}_3, \mathrm{KBr}$, etc. increases appreciable with the increase in temperature. - The solubility of $\mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$ first increase and after $30^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ decreases with the increase in temperature.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Which of the following statement is true for <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>IO</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msubsup><mi mathvariant="normal">F</mi><mn>2</mn><mo>-</mo></msubsup></math>?
options: The electrons are located at the corners of a trigonal bipyramidal but one of the equatorial pairs is unshared.

It has <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>sp</mi><mn>3</mn></msup><mi mathvariant="normal">d</mi></math> hybridisation and is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">T</mi></math>-shaped.
questions.solution:
It has sp3d hybridisation and is see-saw shaped. Since, there is one lone pair present on the central atom i.e., I, and there are four bond pairs. Hence, the structure is similar to SF4.

 (see-saw)
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.q: In the anion <math><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mo>-</mo></mrow></msup></math>, the two carbon-oxygen bonds are found to be of equal length. What is the reason for it?
options: Electron orbits of carbon atom are hybridised.

The <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mo>=</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></math> bond is weaker than the <math><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mo>-</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></math> bond.

The anion <math><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mo>-</mo></mrow></msup></math> has two resonating structures.
The anion is obtained by removal of a proton from the acid molecule.
questions.solution: In HCOO- anion, two carbon-oxygen bonds are of the same bond length as they involve in resonance.

questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.q:
Assertion (A) $\mathrm{Xe}$ atoms in $\mathrm{XeF}_2$ are $d^2 s p^3$ hybridised.
Reason (R) $\mathrm{XeF}_2$ molecule does not follow octet rule.
Which of the following is correct?
options: Both $(A)$ and $(R)$ are true and $(R)$ is the correct explanation of $(A)$
Both $(A)$ and $(R)$ are true, but $(R)$ is not the correct explanation of $(A)$
questions.solution: $\mathrm{XeF}_2$ is $s p^3 d$ hybridised. $\mathrm{XeF}_2$ molecule does not follow octet rule due to expanded octet 10 electrons. Therefore, $A$ is incorrect but $R$ is correct.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Which of the following structures is the most preferred and hence of lowest energy for <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>SO</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math>?
questions.solution: Generally, the lowest energy structure is the one having the smallest formal charges on the atoms. Hence, option 4 is correct. Alternatively, 4th has maximum number of covalent bonds.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If the electronegativity of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mo> </mo></math>and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi></math> are <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>5</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mn>2</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mn>3</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>5</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mn>3</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>0</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>5</mn></math>, respectively, then the correct order of polarity of bonds is:
questions.solution:
Electronegativity of atoms forming a bond is used to determine whether, the given bond will be non-polar covalent, polar covalent or ionic.

It is the ability of an atom in a particular molecule to attract electrons to itself (the greater the value of EN, the greater the force of attraction by atom on electrons).

If electronegativity difference is more, there is more charge separation and thus, bond is more polar.

The electronegativity difference between:

i Sulphur and Oxygen=1

ii Carbon and Nitrogen=0.5

iii Oxygen and Hydrogen=1.4

iv Carbon and Hydrogen=0.4

So, the correct order of the polarity of bonds is:

O-H>S-O>C-N>C-H
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: In the extraction of copper from copper glance, blister copper is formed by the evolution of gas $\underline{X}$. The shape of molecule of $\underline{X}$ is
questions.solution: $2 \mathrm{Cu}_2 \mathrm{~S}+3 \mathrm{O}_2 \rightarrow 2 \mathrm{Cu}_2 \mathrm{O}+2 \mathrm{SO}_2$ $$ 2 \mathrm{Cu}_2 \mathrm{O}+\mathrm{Cu}_2 \mathrm{~S} \rightarrow 6 \mathrm{Cu}+\mathrm{SO}_2 $$ Shape of $\mathrm{SO}_2=$ Angular $/$ bent
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Regarding the structure of <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2 </mn></mrow></msub></math>and <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math> which of the following is true?
options: The gaseous <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math> and <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math> have same V-shaped molecule both in solid and gas phase.

At room temperature both <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math> and <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math> are solids.

In the solid phase the structure of <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math> is <math><mi mathvariant="normal">V</mi></math>-shaped discrete molecule but <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math> is cyclic trimeric.

In solid phase the structure of <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math> is <math><mi mathvariant="normal">V</mi></math>-shaped discrete molecule but <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math> has linear polymeric chain.
questions.solution: The gaseous SO2and solid SO2both are V-shaped discrete molecule. SeO2issolid at room temperature and solid SeO2 in polymerised by infinite chain.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The <math> <mrow> <mtext>N</mtext> <mo>-</mo> <mtext>N</mtext> </mrow> </math> bond length in <math> <mrow> <msub> <mtext>N</mtext> <mn>2</mn> </msub> <mtext>O</mtext> </mrow> </math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo>.12</mo><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">Å</mi></math>, slightly longer than a typical <math> <mrow> <mtext>N</mtext> <mo>≡</mo> <mtext>N</mtext> </mrow> </math> bond and the <math> <mrow> <mtext>N</mtext> <mo>-</mo> <mtext>O</mtext> </mrow> </math> bond length is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo>.19</mo><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">Å</mi></math>, slightly shorter than a typical <math> <mrow> <mtext>N</mtext> <mo>-</mo> <mtext>O</mtext> </mrow> </math> bond.
options: <math> <mrow> <mo>{</mo> <mtable> <mtr> <mtd> <mtext>N</mtext> <mo>-</mo> <mtext>N</mtext> </mtd> <mtd> <mtext>bond order less than 3</mtext> </mtd> </mtr> <mtr> <mtd> <mtext>N</mtext> <mo>-</mo> <mtext>O</mtext> </mtd> <mtd> <mtext>bond order more than 1</mtext> </mtd> </mtr> </mtable> </mrow> </math>

<math> <mrow> <mo>{</mo> <mtable> <mtr> <mtd> <mtext>N</mtext> <mo>-</mo> <mtext>N</mtext> </mtd> <mtd> <mtext>bond order greater than 3</mtext> </mtd> </mtr> <mtr> <mtd> <mtext>N</mtext> <mo>-</mo> <mtext>O</mtext> </mtd> <mtd> <mtext>bond order less than 1</mtext> </mtd> </mtr> </mtable> </mrow> </math>

<math> <mrow> <mo>{</mo> <mtable> <mtr> <mtd> <mtext>N</mtext> <mo>-</mo> <mtext>N</mtext> </mtd> <mtd> <mtext>bond order less than 1</mtext> </mtd> </mtr> <mtr> <mtd> <mtext>N</mtext> <mo>-</mo> <mtext>O</mtext> </mtd> <mtd> <mtext>bond order greater than 1</mtext> </mtd> </mtr> </mtable> </mrow> </math>
questions.solution:
Bond order is defined as the number of bonds present between two bonding atoms in a molecule or compound.  In general, the bond order of N≡N is 3, and that of N-O is 1. Bond length and bond order are inversely proportional to each other. If bond order increases, the bond length decreases, and if bond order decreases, the bond length increases. 

 

Bond length follows the order :

Triple Bond < Double Bond < Single Bond

 

The N2O molecule exhibits the phenomenon of resonance as shown below:

 

Due to the above resonance phenomenon, the bond order of N-N bond becomes 2 (less than 3) and hence, the bond is slightly longer (1.12 Å) than a normal N≡N bond.

 

On the other hand, the bond order of N-O is also 2 (greater than 1) due to the resonance phenomenon. Hence, the bond length is slightly shorter (1.19 Å)  than the typical N-O single bond.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
How many of the following species are diamagnetic?
$$
\mathrm{He}_2^{+}, \mathrm{H}_2, \mathrm{H}_2^{+}, \mathrm{H}_2^{-}, \mathrm{He}
$$
questions.solution:
According to molecular orbital theory, species with all paired electrons in their electronic configuration are diamagnetic in nature.
$\mathrm{He}_2^{+} \rightarrow$ Has total 3 electrons, thus it contains one unpaired electron and is paramagnetic in nature. $\mathrm{H}_2 \rightarrow$ Has total 2 electrons, thus it contains all paired electrons and is diamagnetic in nature. $\mathrm{H}_2^{+} \rightarrow$ Has only l electron, thus it is paramagnetic in nature.
$\mathrm{H}_2^{-} \rightarrow$ Has total 3 electrons, thus it contains one unpaired electron and is paramagnetic in nature. $\mathrm{He} \rightarrow$ Contains total two (2) electrons and has no unpaired electron, thus is diamagnetic in nature.
Hence, among the given species, we have only two of them which are diamagnetic and thus, option (2) is the correct answer.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Be in <math><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math> undergoes
questions.solution:
The electronic configuration of Be is 1s2 2s2 2p0.
In gaseous state BeCl2 exists as linear molecule i..e (Cl-Be-Cl). So, we can say that in BeCl2, the central atom Be undergoes diagonal hybridisation or  sp hybridisation.

Hence, Be in BeCl2 undergoes Diagonal hybridisation.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Compare <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>ZnCl</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>MgCl</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> and find which is more soluble in organic solvents? (atomic number of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Zn</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>30</mn></math> and that of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Mg</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>12</mn></math> )
questions.solution:
As we know that according to solubility rule : Like dissolves like means.

Polar dissolves with polar and non-polar dissolves with non-polar.

Ionic compounds are more polar as compared to covalent compound and water acts as best polar solvent  in which ionic compound will be more soluble in water or in aqueous medium.

According to like dissolve like concept, more covalent is more soluble in organic solvent.

So, ZnCl2 is more covalent as MgCl2.

 Size : rMg+2>rZn+2
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Given below are two statements
Assertion (A): Ionic compounds are formed by nondirectional bonds
Reasoning (R): They are soluble in nonpolar solvents The correct answer is
options: Both (A) and (R) are correct and (R) is the correct explanation of $(A)$
Both (A) and (R) are correct and (R) is not the correct explanation of $(A)$
(A) is correct but (R) is not correct
(A) is not correct but (R) is correct
questions.solution:
Ionic bonds are electrostatic forces of attraction that are non-directional in nature.
Thus, (A) is correct. Ionic compounds are soluble in polar solvents that solvate them via ion-dipole interactions.
Thus, $(\mathrm{R})$ is incorrect.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
